tests.result: Component schedule is explicit, but unresolved physical/contract ownership prevents independent proof of every overlap control.
Material risk remains wherever gross acreage or project economics cannot be traced to one share.
Modeled reserves exist, but enforceable remaining-capital and financing terms are incomplete.
Low/base/high dispersion remains sensitive to duration, unit values, and realization discounts.
Residual risk remains and must not be converted into an approved recommendation.

residual_risks: No project-level ownership and partner waterfall converts gross economics to per-share cash.
No parcel-level map supports a non-overlapping residual-acre balance.
No legal/engineering record supports transferable water quantity, delivery economics, and dated realization.
Related-party note collectability and railway realizability remain insufficiently tested.
strongest_failure_path: Gross collective acreage and project values are attributed to shareholders despite related-entity ownership, cancellable leases, parcel overlap, water restrictions, and illiquid related-party claims; realization takes far longer and costs more than modeled.
